payload.text: Dogfooding finding at initiative creation (2026-07-11): `sofar new` run from an already-bound branch silently steals that branch's binding (notes-in-digest → cli-ui); restored via `sofar switch notes-in-digest`. Candidate CLI-UX fix: warn or require a flag when rebinding a branch that already has a binding.

plan.goal: Build a CLI UI layer for the sofar engine: every command today (status, doctor, new/switch, adopt) prints flat plain text — boring, noisy, and hard to scan. Give CLI output structured terminal rendering: visual hierarchy, color and severity cues, alignment, progress display, less noise so the signal (goal, next action, warnings) stands out. Constraint: terminal rendering only — this is not the guard-railed 'UI' (web/graphical); any new rendering dependency (color/TUI lib) requires a logged Decision first.

Build a CLI UI layer for the sofar engine: every command today (status, doctor, new/switch, adopt) prints flat plain text — boring, noisy, and hard to scan. Give CLI output structured terminal rendering: visual hierarchy, color and severity cues, alignment, progress display, animated per-use-case symbols, less noise so the signal (goal, next action, warnings) stands out. Constraint: terminal rendering only — this is not the guard-railed 'UI' (web/graphical); any new rendering dependency requires a logged Decision first. Agent-facing surfaces (renderStatus digest, hook stdout, export/import NDJSON, mcp stdio) stay byte-identical plain text forever.

tasks.title: Decision: rendering stack — zero-dep hand-rolled kernel (vendored picocolors detection logic + cli-spinners frames, ~130 lines) vs one dep (picocolors); settle and log via sofar_log_decision

cli/ui capability detection + style primitives: color ladder NO_COLOR > --no-color > FORCE_COLOR > isTTY&&TERM!=dumb (+CI), unicode gate w/ ASCII fallbacks, semantic tokens (success/error/warn/info/muted/accent) — ANSI-16 only, no hex for text; tests
Symbols + layout helpers: ✓ ✗ ⚠ ℹ ● [✓]/[•]/[ ] checkboxes, └/│ detail rails, ⋮ elision, … truncation; visible-width pad/truncate (ANSI-strip aware); tests

Spinner kernel on stderr: per-use-case frame sets (scan=braille dots@80ms, write=growVertical, network=point, brand pulse=eased ping-pong ·✢✳✶✻✽ style); animate only isTTY&&!CI, static fallback line otherwise; cursor hide/restore + SIGINT-safe; tests

sofar status styled renderer from InitiativeState (TTY path; plain renderFullStatus stays the piped fallback): header, progress meter, phase tree checkboxes, next-action callout, staleness warning, notes
sofar doctor: Finding levels as ✓/⚠/✗ with section grouping, hints as dim └ lines; scan spinner during tree walk
sofar list: aligned columns (visible-width), semantic coloring, current-branch initiative marker
One-shot confirmations (new/switch/init/uninit/adopt): ✓/✗ result line + dim └ details; upgrade gets network spinner; serve startup banner
SPEC §CLI UI: color law, degradation ladder, flag/env contract, styled-vs-guaranteed-plain surface table, acceptance criteria
Plain-surface regression guard: tests locking renderStatus digest, hook stdout, export/import NDJSON, mcp stdio to byte-identical output regardless of TTY/env
Acceptance suite + live-fire pass: every command in TTY / piped / NO_COLOR / CI modes

payload.chose: Styled static output + stderr spinners at the emit()/renderer boundary, semantic ANSI-16 color law (Codex styles.md model: green=success, red=error, cyan=identifiers, dim=secondary; no hex for text, no bg detection), vendored cli-spinners frame sets per use case, degradation ladder NO_COLOR > --no-color > FORCE_COLOR > TTY, animation only when isTTY && !CI; agent-facing surfaces (renderStatus digest, hook stdout, NDJSON, mcp stdio) guaranteed byte-plain.
payload.over: (a) a TUI framework (Ink/OpenTUI/ratatui class — React+Yoga event loop for one-shot print-and-exit commands, heavy dep-law violation); (b) truecolor themes with terminal background detection (OSC queries are flaky in print-and-exit processes: pipes, CI, tmux); (c) chalk/ora comfort stack (chalk lacks NO_COLOR support entirely; ora is 17 packages for a spinner).
payload.because: Research across Claude Code, Gemini CLI, opencode, Codex CLI, Kiro + Node ecosystem (2026-07-11, three-agent sweep): every fancy animation (Claude's eased ✳ pulse, Codex shimmer, opencode Knight Rider) requires a persistent render loop — wrong shape for sofar's one-shot commands; Codex's own non-interactive mode and the Vite/Astro/Vitest precedent (picocolors or vendored forks) converge on exactly this design; ANSI-16 semantic colors survive every terminal theme without dark/light detection.

payload.text: Design vocabulary adopted from research (reference for Phase 1/2): glyphs ✓ success, ✗ error (+exit code), ⚠ warn, ℹ info, ● state bullets, [✓]/[•]/[ ] task checkboxes, "  └ " detail lines, "  │ " continuation, ⋮ elision, … truncation. Spinner frames (vendor from cli-spinners MIT JSON): dots ⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏@80ms=scan/fold, growVertical ▁▃▄▅▆▇=write, point ∙∙∙●∙∙=network, bouncingBar=ASCII fallback; brand pulse = Claude-style eased ping-pong (raised-cosine ~2s, e.g. ·✢✳✶✻✽). ASCII symbol fallbacks via 15-line is-unicode-supported gate (√ × ‼ i). Spinners → stderr; static ⋯ line when !TTY/CI. commander v15 already ships styled --help (NO_COLOR/FORCE_COLOR-aware) — free. Node util.styleText needs ≥20.12, off-table at Node18 floor.

plan.goal: Build a CLI UI layer for the sofar engine: every command today (status, doctor, new/switch, adopt) prints flat plain text — boring, noisy, and hard to scan. Give CLI output structured terminal rendering: visual hierarchy, color and severity cues, alignment, progress display, animated per-use-case symbols, less noise so the signal (goal, next action, warnings) stands out. The layout must render the initiative object model (initiative → goal → phases → tasks → next action) at two zoom levels: single initiative (status) and multi-initiative portfolio (list). Constraint: terminal rendering only — this is not the guard-railed 'UI' (web/graphical); any new rendering dependency requires a logged Decision first. Agent-facing surfaces (renderStatus digest, hook stdout, export/import NDJSON, mcp stdio) stay byte-identical plain text forever.

tasks.title: Decision: rendering stack — zero-dep hand-rolled kernel (vendored picocolors detection logic + cli-spinners frames, ~130 lines) vs one dep (picocolors); settle and log via sofar_log_decision

cli/ui capability detection + style primitives: color ladder NO_COLOR > --no-color > FORCE_COLOR > isTTY&&TERM!=dumb (+CI), unicode gate w/ ASCII fallbacks, semantic tokens (success/error/warn/info/muted/accent) — ANSI-16 only, no hex for text; tests
Symbols + layout helpers: ✓ ✗ ⚠ ℹ ● [✓]/[•]/[ ] checkboxes, └/│ detail rails, ⋮ elision, … truncation; visible-width pad/truncate (ANSI-strip aware); tests

Spinner kernel on stderr: per-use-case frame sets (scan=braille dots@80ms, write=growVertical, network=point, brand pulse=eased ping-pong ·✢✳✶✻✽ style); animate only isTTY&&!CI, static fallback line otherwise; cursor hide/restore + SIGINT-safe; tests

Layout grammar for the initiative object model: one visual hierarchy (initiative header → goal → phase tree → tasks → next action → warnings) rendered at two zoom levels — full block (status: one initiative) and portfolio block/row (list: many initiatives, each with own goal/phases/progress); shared components so both surfaces stay visually congruent; tests

sofar status styled renderer = full-zoom grammar applied to InitiativeState (TTY path; plain renderFullStatus stays the piped fallback): header, progress meter, phase tree checkboxes, next-action callout, staleness warning, notes

sofar list styled renderer = portfolio-zoom grammar: per-initiative block (slug, bound branch, progress, active phase, next-action one-liner), aligned visible-width columns, current-branch marker; presentation only — what the surface lists is owned by the initiative-list initiative
sofar doctor: Finding levels as ✓/⚠/✗ with section grouping, hints as dim └ lines; scan spinner during tree walk
One-shot confirmations (new/switch/init/uninit/adopt): ✓/✗ result line + dim └ details; upgrade gets network spinner; serve startup banner
SPEC §CLI UI: color law, degradation ladder, flag/env contract, styled-vs-guaranteed-plain surface table, acceptance criteria
Plain-surface regression guard: tests locking renderStatus digest, hook stdout, export/import NDJSON, mcp stdio to byte-identical output regardless of TTY/env
Acceptance suite + live-fire pass: every command in TTY / piped / NO_COLOR / CI modes

payload.text: User design guidance (2026-07-11): checkmarks carry semantic color (green ✓ done, amber • active, dim ○/[ ] pending — opencode's todo treatment); the currently-active task/phase should read as 'working' via spinner or color pulse. Constraint to honor in design: status is print-and-exit — animation can't outlive the process, so active tasks get a color-pulse STATIC glyph in the tree; live spinners/pulses apply while a command is actually running (doctor scan, upgrade network) or in a future live surface (--watch / serve-backed).

payload.chose: Zero-dependency hand-rolled UI kernel in packages/engine/src/cli/ui/: vendor picocolors' color-detection + formatter-nesting logic (~40 lines), cli-spinners frame data as typed constants, and the is-unicode-supported gate (~15 lines) — all MIT, attributed in source comments.
payload.over: Adding picocolors as a dependency (1 pkg, 32 KB, zero transitive — the Vite/Next.js route); chalk/ora comfort stack (already rejected in D1: chalk lacks NO_COLOR, ora is 17 packages); Node's util.styleText (needs >=20.12, repo floor is Node 18).
payload.because: Keeps the dependency ledger untouched entirely (repo law treats every new dep as a Decision-worthy event; zero-dep needs no ongoing supply-chain trust); Vitest (tinyrainbow) and Astro (piccolore) set the precedent of vendoring picocolors-class logic; ~130 lines total sits comfortably inside this repo's test discipline; and we control NO_COLOR > FORCE_COLOR precedence spec-compliantly, which ansis and util.styleText get wrong.

payload.note: Zero-dep vendored kernel chosen; decision logged (D2).
caps.ts + style.ts, 16 tests, committed f65fedb on cli-ui branch (isolated worktree).
symbols.ts + text.ts, 7 more tests (23 total), committed 542a0ed.
frames.ts + spinner.ts + ui/index.ts barrel, 27 kernel tests, full suite 431 green, committed 74da4a2. Phase 1 complete.

payload.summary: Phase 1 built and verified: 1.1 Decision D2 (zero-dep vendored kernel), 1.2 caps+style, 1.3 symbols+text, 1.4 spinner+frames — 27 kernel tests, full suite 431 green, live-fire demo OK. Work on cli-ui branch in isolated worktree .claude/worktrees/cli-ui (shared checkout carries a live concurrent session on initiative-list — untouched). Pushed origin/cli-ui (f65fedb..74da4a2).
payload.next_action: Phase 2: start 2.1 layout grammar (initiative object model at status/list zoom levels) in the cli-ui worktree, then wire status (2.2).

payload.text: Release ruling (user, Jul 11): publish is deferred until cli-ui completes — one release will ship notes-in-digest + initiative-list + cli-ui together. Global install sits at 0.5.0 until then (known deploy lag; merged features are main-only). Whoever finishes cli-ui: bump + publish + sofar upgrade is part of the wrap-up.

payload.note: Phase 2 launched as a multi-agent workflow (user-requested): 2.1 gates 2.2/2.3; 2.4/2.5 parallel; verify → review → fix → commit stages.
